evaluations.notes: Passes hard gates with complete nodes, directions, editable draw.io source, derived chain, and planned validation. Layout grouping is adequate but less explicit for service/security separation and PowerPoint path-length is only covered as path issues.

Strong structure and validation plan. Includes all required graph content and provenance. Slightly weaker grouping coverage for service/security as independent layout areas and path-length is implicit through path issues.
Complete structured input, artifact chain, boundary handling, and planned versus completed validation. Layout puts power/security to the right rather than ideal grouped areas but remains usable.
Good semantic coverage and clear no-execution boundary. Minor weakness: blocker wording claims Mermaid content is included and PowerPoint path-length is not explicit beyond path problems.

Very complete and repeatable. Covers graph schema, layout, source-of-truth rule, artifact provenance, validation boundary, and blockers. PowerPoint path-length is covered through path/export issues rather than named exactly.

High-quality answer with clear schema, precise graph content, grouped layout, provenance, validation distinction, and blockers. Path-length is not named exactly but local file path/export blockers are covered.

Excellent structure, provenance, validation distinction, and stop conditions. Layout grouping is compact and usable. Does not explicitly say path-length, but path-dependent PowerPoint failure is covered.

Best overall semantic package: strong structured input, grouped layout, exact graph, strict provenance, planned/completed validation split, and clear blocker handling. Path/export issues are covered though path-length is not named exactly.

Complete, concise, and repeatable. Covers required graph, source-of-truth rule, export chain, planned validation, and blockers. Slightly less explicit about one page in the YAML schema and path-length as a distinct limit.
Strong candidate with clear graph schema, layout, provenance, planned validation, and stop conditions. The PowerPoint path/permission issue is acceptable but not specifically framed as path-length.
Passes core gates and is concise. Weaker than top candidates because grouping/layout hints are less explicit in the structured input and Mermaid parsing is introduced as a dependency.
Strong prompt-only package with clear validation separation and artifact provenance. Slightly weaker on explicit export metadata naming and path-length wording.

quality_winner_reason: Lowest-cost profile that passes all hard gates and quality thresholds; higher-cost candidates improve polish but not enough to justify selection under the cost-after-quality rule.
